characters.name: "Ace" Weems
characters.description: "Ace" Weems. District director of theTarpaulin Insurance Company. He got the nickname "Ace" when he and hispoker-playing buddies "King" Garrity and "Jack" Palermo began naming themselves after the cards ("It was either that or 'Queen'"). After sixCONTROLagentsdie in unusual circumstances"Ace" WeemsinformsMaxwell Smartand theChiefthat all were former members of theCONTROL Training Schoolbaseball team. The culprit is deemed to beAdrian Listenger, a former CONTROL trainee and catcher on the team, seeking revenge after being forced to drop out of the spy school because one of the team members reported that he had cheated on a test.

characters.name: "Ozark Annie" Jones
characters.description: Ozark Annie. Entertainer who claims to have "millions of devoted followers and fans" although precisely what she does is never clearly explained. Annie inadvertently swallows an olive containing a newKAOScommunications device. She is later kidnapped bySiegfriedwho is intent on cutting it out of her.

characters.name: "Tiger" Schlermer
characters.description: "Tiger" Schlermer in the boxing ring. CONTROLagentwho's cover is being a professional boxer. Schlermer isMaxwell Smart's fourth choice to be Best Man at his wedding but is prevented from doing so when he falls into an open man-hole and suffers a concussion.

characters.name: 99's Bridesmaid
characters.description: 99's bridesmaid (standing, in green dress). 99's bridesmaid is a woman about whom absolutely nothing is known other than that she is married toHerbertwho accompanies her. Her status is inferred by the fact that she can be seen standing at the end of the aisle during the wedding ceremony holding a bouquet.

characters.name: 99's Mother
characters.description: 99's Mother meets Max. Like herdaughterandgranddaughter, the name of "99's Mother" is never revealed. She is a widow; her late husband, "a strange man but a good provider", was a spy. However, despite a number of obvious parallels - he talked into his suspenders rather than his shoe but, likeMax, claimed to be a greeting card salesman - she never seems to guess Max and99's true occupation. The only reference to her relationship with her late husband is when she knocks outKAOSAgentSimon the Likeablewith a right cross punch to the jaw on the grounds he resembles her late and unlamented husband! [Episode #120: "And Baby Makes Four, conclusion".]

characters.name: Abe Fu Yung
characters.description: Abe Fu Yung. KAOSagent; henchman ofThe Whip. Born in New York's lower east side, as a childFuwas always getting into fights because of his first name. Bullies were always beating him up and then he was coming back again in a half hour for more.

characters.name: Adam
characters.description: KAOS Agent #1 surveils Max at an illegal "gambling casino". Initially unnamed member of a group ofKAOSagents plotting to blow up thePentagon. AfterMaxwell Smartinfiltrates the group he learns that they are all actually Western undercover agents, at which point Adam reveals his name and introduces himself asCIA.

characters.name: Admiral Crichton
characters.description: Admiral Crichton. Military officer working withGeneral ChristianandColonel Quintoncoordinating an operation to stopProfessor Stanislavsky's mad scheme of freezing over the world using theStanislavsky Method. Portrayed byDel Moore[Episode #125: "Ice Station Siegfried"].

characters.name: Admiral Reefer
characters.description: Dr. Braam listens to the admiral's tale of woe. Patient of Washington psychiatristDr. Myron Braam, who is secretly aKAOSagent. After catching his secretaryJoanna Sloanexamining Reefer's file, Braam orders his henchmanGeorge Markovichto "take care of her' by trapping her in a phone booth which then fills with water while she is trying to alertMaxwell Smartto Braam's treachery.

characters.name: Agent 0
characters.description: The faces of Agent 0. Spoken as "Agent Zero". CONTROLdisguise expertwho adopts a different persona in every appearance.

characters.name: Agent 107
characters.description: "107" is whatMaxwell Smartcalls "Tiger" Schlermer when he asks him to be Best Man at his wedding. However, according to information recorded on a blackboard by99, Schlermer's number is14. Since the only other three-digit agent numbers known are those of rookie agentsLundyandCaruso- 198 and 199 respectively - it may be speculated that 107 was Schlermer's rookie number. Or maybe Max just made a mistake.

characters.description: Agent 11may refer to:

characters.name: Agent 12
characters.description: Max makes contact with Agent 12. The lowest-numberedCONTROLagent seen on active duty in the original series (other than the interstitially sequencedAgent 8 1/2). Agent 12 is seen dressed asSanta ClausatBowers Department Store, aKAOSfront managed byConrad Bunny. He (along with other CONTROL agents planted in the store) is said to have been "taken care of", if not by Bunny himself then possibly by his henchmanGorcheck. However, no body is seen [Episode #4: "Our Man in Toyland"].

characters.name: Agent 13 (comic strip)
characters.description: Agents 13 and 18 lament. CONTROLagentwho, along withAgent 18, believe they have failed in their assignment in the Monte Carlo casino case, and attempts suicide as a result. Note: This character seems not to be the sameAgent 13seen in thetelevision seriessince when encountered bySmartand99they appear unknown to each other.

characters.name: Agent 14
characters.description: CONTROLagentmentioned in testimony given by thefake Smartto the board of inquiry set up to determine which is the realMaxwell Smartafter an impostor shows up at CONTROL headquarters [Episode #63: "The Spy Who Met Himself"]. Agent 14 is later identified as"Tiger" Schlermer- Max's fourth choice to be Best Man at his wedding [Portrayed byRon Masak, Episode #92: "The Worst Best Man"].

characters.name: Agent 17
characters.description: An Agent 17 is seen on three occasions but it is unclear whether any or all of them might be the same man: Agent 17 disguised as a monkey.

characters.name: Agent 22 (PITS)
characters.description: PITSagentassigned to work withMaxwell Smartto thwart aKAOSthreat to destroy all fabric thereby rendering the whole world nude. 22 is the widow of the late, greatAgent 78. According to13, 22 was 21 when she married 78 in '72, and joined PITS when 78 died in '75 (investigating the numbers racket). She majored in vanishing at theSpy Academyand is an excellent marksperson, being able, at a distance, to prevent Max from mistakenly shooting at her at their first meeting by putting her bullet in his barrel - much to Max's annoyance. Despite this less than perfect introduction, 22 soon finds herself impressed with Max's response when KAOS attacks them with the old big, black truck trick - falling over backwards, allowing the truck to pass over him and coming up shooting - even when he explains that he slipped on a banana peel.
